case_name: Bryant v. Wake Forest Univ. Baptist Med. Ctr.

summary.brief: Bryant v. Wake Forest Univ. Baptist Med. Ctr.. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: In 2007, Plaintiff was referred to Mehmet Tamer Yalcinkaya, M.D. by a large uterine myoma, also known as a uterine fibroid.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: After examining Plaintiff, Defendant Yalcinkaya confirmed her uterine myoma diagnosis and recommended an exploratory laparotomy (abdominal surgery) and myomectomy (surgical removal of uterine fibroids).
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Defendant Yalcinkaya performed the surgery on 5 October 2007.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Defendant Yalcinkaya determined that Plaintiff had Stage IV endometriosis, an advanced form of a disorder that results in abnormal endometrial tissue growth outside the uterus.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Near the end of the surgery, Defendant Yalcinkaya implanted a prelude peritoneal membrane, also known as a Gore-Tex adhesion barrier, to prevent adhesions from forming at the surgical incision site.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The use of the Gore-Tex barrier was documented in Defendant Yalcinkaya’s operative note for the procedure, as well as in the perioperative record of the procedure.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On 12 March 2020, Plaintiff’s standard of care expert, Steven D. McCarus, M.D. (“Dr. McCarus”), was deposed.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Gore-Tex barriers “can” have a therapeutic purpose “if they are properly used. In the case of Kimberly Bryant, the Gore-Tex adhesion barriers were improperly used because Dr. Yalcinkaya failed to remove them within 2 to 8 weeks after the gynecologic surgery.”
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. McCarus

evidence_index.text: We affirm the trial court’s grant of summary judgment for each of Plaintiff’s claims.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Summary judgment is proper “if the pleadings, depositions, answers to interrogatories, and admissions on file, together with the affidavits, if any, show that there is no genuine issue as to any material fact and that any party is entitled to a judgment as a matter of law.”
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

evidence_index.text: Therefore, because we hold that the trial court properly granted summary judgment on each of Plaintiff’s claims above, Plaintiff has no independent basis for punitive damages and this claim necessarily fails.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient was referred to Dr. Yalcinkaya due to pelvic pain caused by a large uterine myoma.
initial_state.initial_working_problem: Large uterine myoma causing pelvic pain

requestables.name: Operative Note
requestables.description: Details of the surgical procedure performed by Dr. Yalcinkaya

reveal.result_summary: Gore-Tex barrier implanted, fibroid and adhesions removed
reveal.clinical_significance: Implantation of Gore-Tex barrier to prevent adhesions
reveal.decision_impact: Implantation intended to prevent adhesions and improve fertility

reveal.result_summary: Recommendation for drug therapy and possible second surgery
reveal.clinical_significance: Further treatment needed for endometriosis and fibroid management
reveal.decision_impact: Patient did not follow up with recommended treatments

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with pelvic pain due to uterine myoma

timeline_phases.name: Post-operative Follow-up
timeline_phases.description: Follow-up visits and recommendations for further treatment

key_events.event: Recommendation for drug therapy and second surgery

decision_points.clinical_context: During surgery for uterine fibroid removal
decision_points.prompt: Should the Gore-Tex barrier be left permanently?

options.clinical_reasoning: Intended to prevent adhesions and improve fertility

options.description: Plan to remove Gore-Tex barrier within 2-8 weeks
options.clinical_reasoning: Proper use requires removal to prevent complications

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Believed it would prevent adhesions and improve fertility

expected_action_court.description: Gore-Tex barrier should have been removed within 2-8 weeks
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated removal was necessary

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Leaving the barrier permanently was not therapeutic
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Barrier should have been removed to prevent complications
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed a large pelvic mass requiring further surgery
patient_outcome.severity: Significant complications due to retained Gore-Tex barrier

key_findings: Gore-Tex barrier had a therapeutic purpose at implantation

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Alleged failure to remove Gore-Tex barrier
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Barrier removal might have prevented complications

factual_timeline.event: Patient referred to Dr. Yalcinkaya for uterine myoma
Exploratory laparotomy by Dr. Johnston, Gore-Tex barrier removed

court_findings.finding: No breach of duty of care
